mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates directly to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting equity markets over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of low unemployment with market strength
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is exceptionally low while retail sales (RSAFS) are growing strongly, it indicates ample liquidity supporting consumer spending, which fuels corporate profits and drives equity valuations higher over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and RSAFS shows strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production (INDPRO), a sustained increase indicates strong manufacturing activity and corporate investment, leading to higher earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, especially during recovery phases.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth during identified post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold signals overheating in the economy, which typically leads to tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, negatively impacting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer confidence and spending, thereby contributing positively to corporate earnings and short-term stock market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:employment reports often move markets on release
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns for S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are strong and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, it indicates robust economic activity driven by both consumer and investment demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading indicator of economic health
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where HOUST is above 1.5M units and UNRATE is below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a rising Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon. This effect is particularly pronounced when rates are already elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:historical impact of rate hikes on equity markets
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25% over three months during a tightening cycle.
